action.reasoning: Observation: Latest training achieves solid val R2 (t:0.67, q:0.31, rad:0.99, precip:0.79, mean:0.69) and improved test metrics on t/rad/precip but ptend_q remains catastrophic on test (-2.14e8 vs val 0.31), driving mean_r2 ~-9e7 (worse than iter3). Predict batching fixed, training stable with weighted loss/clipping. Analysis: Clear generalization gap isolated to q-tendency (likely unstable large errors on test dist.); val selection on weighted-MSE not aligned with mean_r2; current capacity/init allows val fit but not robust preds. Stronger reg (dropout, WD, smaller LR/output-init, Kaiming), higher q-weight=20, best-ckpt on mean_val_r2, deeper net should target q without hurting others. 35 epochs ~25min feasible. Decision: Update model.py with inits, refactor train.py for per-epoch R2 logging+selection on mean_r2 + hypers, keep predict.py, train then submit_eval on best.pt.
